title: Identifying Viral Sequences Using VirSorter (Cyverse)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"g23c","text":"Identifying putative viral sequences from SPAdes-assembled data from the Ocean Sampling Day (2014) metagenomic datasets using VirSorter.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":12,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":73,"length":25}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"94C346AFED5C4D19AFD8215A0E1B4FBB","url":"https://github.com/MicroB3-IS/osd-analysis"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"crjss","text":"This is part of a larger protocol Collection that involves the end-to-end processing of raw viral metagenomic reads obtained from a sequencing facility to assembly and analysis using Apps (i.e. tools) developed by iVirus and implemented within the Cyverse cyberinfrastructure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":34,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqugu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3nq1k","text":"Finding putative viral sequences is one spring board for viral metagenomic analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1sm2k","text":"To run this protocol, users must first register for Cyverse account. All data (both inputs and outputs) are available within Cyverse's data store at /iplant/home/shared/iVirus/ExampleData/","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":39,"length":8}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6D256E3FABA1413EAE2E3D5A12FAEFFA","url":"https://user.iplantcollaborative.org/register/"}}}}
steps: Open VirSorter Open VirSorter from 'Apps' a

Select Inputs Select the 'Section 1' tab. For Input Fasta file of microbial contig Sequences Navigate to Community Data -- > iVirus --> ExampleData --> VirSorter --> Input . Select contigs.fasta Alternatively, copy-and-paste the location: /iplant/home/shared/iVirus/ExampleData/VirSorter/Input into the navigation bar and select the contigs fasta file. For Database Select Virome . There are only two databases to select. Virome and RefSeq. a

Select Parameters Select the 'Section 2' tab. The default options in this section will be sufficient. Additional viral sequence to be used as reference : Here users can select a fasta file that includes viruses that may not be in either 'Virome' or 'RefSeq' databases. For example, unpublished viral sequences used internally in a lab. Virome Decontamination : Virsorter was designed to screen for viral sequences within microbial datasets. Part of its methodology identifies the 'non-viral' component and uses that as background to improve differentiation between viral and non-viral sequences. If most and/or all of the sequences are potentially viral, select this option. VirSorter will use a 'generic' cellular dataset as background. a
Launch Analysis Run the job! VirSorter can take minutes, hours or days depending on the size of your dataset. For this example it should take an afternoon.

Results Expected results can be found from the 'Outputs' directory of VirSorter. Navigate to Community Data -- > iVirus --> ExampleData --> VirSorter --> Output . Alternatively, copy-and-paste the location: /iplant/home/shared/iVirus/ExampleData/VirSorter/Output into the navigation bar. a
creator: Benjamin Bolduc
